Problem Statement: For the following exercises, for each polynomial, a. find the degree; b. find the zeros, if any; c. find the $$y$$ -intercept(s), if any; d. use the leading coefficient to determine the graph’s end behavior; and e. determine algebraically whether the polynomial is even, odd, or neither.
Question: $$f ( x ) = 2 x^{2} - 3 x - 5$$
Answer: a. 2 b. $$\frac{5}{2} , -1 ;$$ c. -5 d. Both ends rise e. Neither

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, for each polynomial, a. find the degree; b. find the zeros, if any; c. find the $$y$$ -intercept(s), if any; d. use the leading coefficient to determine the graph’s end behavior; and e. determine algebraically whether the polynomial is even, odd, or neither.
Question: $$f ( x ) = -3 x^{2} + 6 x$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, for each polynomial, a. find the degree; b. find the zeros, if any; c. find the $$y$$ -intercept(s), if any; d. use the leading coefficient to determine the graph’s end behavior; and e. determine algebraically whether the polynomial is even, odd, or neither.
Question: $$f ( x ) = \frac{1}{2} x^{2} - 1$$
Answer: a. 2 b. $$\pm \sqrt{2}$$ c. -1 d. Both ends rise e. Even

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, for each polynomial, a. find the degree; b. find the zeros, if any; c. find the $$y$$ -intercept(s), if any; d. use the leading coefficient to determine the graph’s end behavior; and e. determine algebraically whether the polynomial is even, odd, or neither.
Question: $$f ( x ) = 3 x - x^{3}$$
Answer: a. 3 b. 0, $$\pm \sqrt{3}$$ c. 0 d. Left end rises, right end falls e. Odd

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether the statement is true or false . Explain why.
Question: $$f ( x ) = ( 4 x + 1 ) \text{/} ( 7 x - 2 )$$ is a transcendental function.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether the statement is true or false . Explain why.
Question: $$g ( x ) = \sqrt[3]{x}$$ is an odd root function

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether the statement is true or false . Explain why.
Question: A logarithmic function is an algebraic function.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether the statement is true or false . Explain why.
Question: A function of the form $$f ( x ) = x^{b} ,$$ where $$b$$ is a real valued constant, is an exponential function.
Answer: False; $$f ( x ) = x^{b} ,$$ where $$b$$ is a real-valued constant, is a power function

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether the statement is true or false . Explain why.
Question: The domain of an even root function is all real numbers.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether the statement is true or false . Explain why.
Question: [T] A company purchases some computer equipment for $20,500. At the end of a 3-year period, the value of the equipment has decreased linearly to $12,300. Find a function $$y = V ( t )$$ that determines the value V of the equipment at the end of t years. Find and interpret the meaning of the $$x$$ - and $$y$$ -intercepts for this situation. What is the value of the equipment at the end of 5 years? When will the value of the equipment be $3000?
Answer: a. $$V ( t ) = -2733 t + 20500$$ b. $$( 0 , 20 , 500 )$$ means that the initial purchase price of the equipment is $20,500; $$( 7.5 , 0 )$$ means that in 7.5 years the computer equipment has no value. c. $6835 d. In approximately 6.4 years

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether the statement is true or false . Explain why.
Question: [T] Total online shopping during the Christmas holidays has increased dramatically during the past 5 years. In 2012 $$\text{(} t = 0 \text{),}$$ total online holiday sales were $42.3 billion, whereas in 2013 they were $48.1 billion. Find a linear function S that estimates the total online holiday sales in the year t . Interpret the slope of the graph of S . Use part a. to predict the year when online shopping during Christmas will reach $60 billion.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether the statement is true or false . Explain why.
Question: [T] A family bakery makes cupcakes and sells them at local outdoor festivals. For a music festival, there is a fixed cost of $125 to set up a cupcake stand. The owner estimates that it costs $0.75 to make each cupcake. The owner is interested in determining the total cost $$C$$ as a function of number of cupcakes made. Find a linear function that relates cost C to x , the number of cupcakes made. Find the cost to bake 160 cupcakes. If the owner sells the cupcakes for $1.50 apiece, how many cupcakes does she need to sell to start making profit? ( Hint : Use the INTERSECTION function on a calculator to find this number.)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether the statement is true or false . Explain why.
Question: [T] A house purchased for $250,000 is expected to be worth twice its purchase price in 18 years. Find a linear function that models the price P of the house versus the number of years t since the original purchase. Interpret the slope of the graph of P . Find the price of the house 15 years from when it was originally purchased.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether the statement is true or false . Explain why.
Question: [T] A car was purchased for $26,000. The value of the car depreciates by $1500 per year. Find a linear function that models the value V of the car after t years. Find and interpret $$V ( 4 ) .$$
Answer: a. $$V ( t ) = -1500 t + 26,000$$ b. In 4 years, the value of the car is $20,000.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether the statement is true or false . Explain why.
Question: [T] A condominium in an upscale part of the city was purchased for $432,000. In 35 years it is worth $60,500. Find the rate of depreciation.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether the statement is true or false . Explain why.
Question: [T] The total cost C to produce a certain item is modeled by the function $$C ( x ) = 10.50 x + 28,500 ,$$ where x is the number of items produced. Determine the cost to produce 175 items.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether the statement is true or false . Explain why.
Question: [T] A professor asks her class to report the amount of time t they spent writing two assignments. Most students report that it takes them about 45 minutes to type a four-page assignment and about 1.5 hours to type a nine-page assignment. Find the linear function $$y = N ( t )$$ that models this situation, where $$N$$ is the number of pages typed and t is the time in minutes. Use part a. to determine how many pages can be typed in 2 hours. Use part a. to determine how long it takes to type a 20-page assignment.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether the statement is true or false . Explain why.
Question: [T] The output (as a percent of total capacity) of nuclear power plants in the United States can be modeled by the function $$P ( t ) = 1.8576 t + 68.052 ,$$ where t is time in years and $$t = 0$$ corresponds to the beginning of 2000. Use the model to predict the approximate percentage output at the beginning of 2015.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether the statement is true or false . Explain why.
Question: [T] The admissions office at a public university estimates that 65% of the students offered admission to the class of 2019 will actually enroll. Find the linear function $$y = N ( x ) ,$$ where $$N$$ is the number of students that actually enroll and $$x$$ is the number of all students offered admission to the class of 2019. If the university wants the 2019 freshman class size to be 1350, determine how many students should be offered admission.
